title: iberis pectinata var. bourgaei
intro: Iberis pectinata var. bourgaei, also known as the rock cress, is a type of flowering plant in the mustard family that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. Rock cress has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to its potential interactions with other medications.
